text: In one sentence, what's the specific service you'll deliver?
subtext: e.g., 'Weekly residential lawn mowing including edging and green-waste removal.'
why: A specific description of your service lets us judge demand and find realistic local competitors.

text: How wide is the area you'll serve?
why: How wide an area you'll serve changes your travel time, how many competitors you're up against, and how big the market is.

option_notes.Single suburb / neighbourhood: We'll keep competitor and demand research tight and local, with minimal travel time built in.
option_notes.City-wide: We'll widen the competitor search and factor in more travel time between jobs.
option_notes.Metro + surrounds (up to ~50km): We'll factor in longer travel times and a broader, more competitive local market.
option_notes.Regional (multiple towns): We'll factor in significant travel between towns and a more spread-out, lower-density customer base.

subtext: e.g., 'Busy families with front-and-back yards in single-storey homes.'
why: A clear picture of your ideal customer sharpens the demand and pricing research in your report.
How you charge changes your cash flow and what pricing benchmarks we compare you against.

option_notes.Per hour: We'll compare you against typical hourly rates in your trade and area.
option_notes.Per visit / per job (flat): We'll look at typical flat per-job pricing so you're not under- or over-charging.
option_notes.Per project (quoted): We'll cover how to scope and quote jobs accurately, since pricing varies job to job.
option_notes.Monthly subscription / retainer: We'll model steady recurring revenue and typical retainer pricing in your trade.
option_notes.Package (bundle of visits): We'll look at how bundled pricing affects your cash flow and customer commitment.

text: Roughly what price do you have in mind (in your local currency)?
subtext: Whatever fits the model above — hourly rate, per-job fee, monthly retainer. Leave blank if unsure.
why: Your target price tells us if you're pricing in line with the local market. Example figures here are just examples — your report prices everything in your own currency, using costs for your country.

text: How much time will you put into this each week?
why: How much time you can commit each week shapes how many clients or jobs are realistic to take on.

text: How much money do you have to start?
subtext: For a vehicle, tools, insurance, marketing. An exact figure or a best guess both work.
why: Your budget shapes what tools and insurance are realistic from day one — and an exact figure makes the report's budget-fit verdict exact too. Enter it in your own currency; the report prices everything using costs for your country.
